name: Low Cal Baked Beef Stew with Sour Cream Biscuits
recipeIngredient: 3/4 to 1 lb. boneless beef top round steak, cut into 1/2-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. In a plastic bag, combine beef and 1 tablespoon flour. Shake to coat.
2. Spray Dutch oven with Pam.
3. Add meat and chopped onion to the Dutch oven. Cook until brown and tender.
4. Drain any fat.
5. Add potatoes, Italian stewed tomatoes (undrained), tomato paste, 2 cups water, beef bouillon granules, sugar, thyme, Worcestershire sauce, and bay leaf.
6. Bake or cook on top of stove, covered, at 350°F for 1 hour.
7. Add eggplant; cover and bake for an additional 30 minutes.
8. Meanwhile, in a large bowl, stir together flour and baking powder. Cut in margarine until crumbly.
9. Stir in 1/3 cup low-fat sour cream and skim milk. On lightly floured board, knead dough 8-10 times.
10. Roll or pat to 1/2-inch thick. Using a 2-inch biscuit cutter, cut into 10-12 circles.
11. Remove stew from oven; increase temperature to 425°F.
12. Discard bay leaf.
13. Combine 1/3 cup low-fat sour cream and flour. Stir into stew.
14. Brush biscuits with milk. Sprinkle with sesame seeds.
15. Arrange on top of meat.
16. Bake, uncovered, for 20-25 minutes until golden. (Optional: Dust with thyme.)
17. Final step with biscuits must be done in the oven.
